title: Using extra_hosts results in blank /etc/resolv.conf in the container.
body:
### Issue Description

Using extra_hosts in compose file results in blank /etc/resolv.conf in the container with no errors.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. define extra_hosts for container
```
extra_hosts:
  - "admin.dev.local:192.168.200.100"
```


### Describe the results you received

/etc/resolv.conf is empty

### Describe the results you expected

/etc/resolv.conf file is not empty

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

Same file works on docker.
Empty /etc/resolve.conf causes "Could not resolve host" error.

comments.body:
extra host has nothing to do with resolv.conf so I don't see how this could ever happen.
Do you have a reproducer without compose? Either via cli or like curl API query?

Ok, You're right, it's only, when in extra_hosts is "host.docker.internal:host-gateway". From cli there is an error but not from compose and in result /etc/resolv.conf is empty.
Now I can see that there is already closed issue with that: #22881 :(

> https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22881

That is entirely different context. For macos there is https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21681 but that should cause a hard error when starting the container and is not related to resolv.conf at all. You don't provide a meaningful reproducer here so it is impossible to guess what you are actually doing.

If you manage to start a container without an error in such case and with an empty resolv.conf I can only guess that the network setup was not executed which would be a rather bad bug so a proper full reproducer would be helpful to clarify that.

Ok, I've managed to reproduce it every time and now I know that there is no error when you simply run `podman compose up`. The error `unable to replace "host-gateway"` only occurs when using the -d (detach) flag. docker-compose.yml: ```yaml services: php-test: image: php:8.3-fpm-alpine3.20 mem_limit: "4G" container_name: php-test extra_hosts: - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway" networks: test_net: ipv4_address: 192.168.100.150 networks: test_net: driver: bridge driver_opts: com.docker.network.enable_ipv6: "false" ipam: driver: default config: - subnet: 192.168.100.0/24 ``` With `podman compose up` both files `/etc/resolv.conf` and `/etc/hosts` are empty:

I can reproduce by setting `host_containers_internal_ip = "none"` in containers.conf on linux to trigger the same condition as on podman-machine on macos.  On the server log I see
```
ERRO[0100] Error attaching to container 81222f148272b104a48540361936871f3f962c178fee604a6ce4b0dba46ece48: preparing container 81222f148272b104a48540361936871f3f962c178fee604a6ce4b0dba46ece48 for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "host.docker.internal:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty 
```
Running `compose up -d` returns the error back top the client correctly.

The fact that the container is started regardless is concerning and definitely a bug. Something around the compat API attach logic seems wrong here.
cc @mheon

Per the code, we're not reporting the error to the client as the hijack has already occurred. I can see why it was written this way, but we probably should be writing it to the hijacked connection as well as just to the server logs.

As to why the container is still started, I'm fairly certain that's not the server. REST API attach will never start the container. 

What I think is going on is this: Client attaches before starting the container to ensure that no output is missed. This failed due to the aforementioned /etc/hosts error, but the client doesn't notice because it happens after the hijack hence no failure code. Client requests container be started. Container start succeeds, despite attach failing. I think the container was transitioned to the Created state as part of `initUnlocked()` during attach, and when the error occurred we did not transition it back to Initialized despite the failure, so the Start API call goes ahead and starts the container despite the earlier failure.

yes that sounds plausible, AFAICS we do not explicitly signal that we are hijacked yet, the init (and thus network setup) must happen before so the fact the attach does not report the error is the main issue here I think.

So interestingly enough I did a quick check by attaching via curl and some debugs points on the server

```
$ curl -s -v --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock -X POST "http://localhost/containers/php-test/attach?stream=true&
stdout=true"
*   Trying /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Connected to localhost (/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) port 0
> POST /containers/php-test/attach?stream=true&stdout=true HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost
> User-Agent: curl/8.9.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Request completely sent off
< HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
< Api-Version: 1.41
< Content-Type: application/json
< Libpod-Api-Version: 5.6.0-dev
< Server: Libpod/5.6.0-dev (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc00008c0c8
< Date: Fri, 16 May 2025 15:33:22 GMT
< Content-Length: 435
< 
{"cause":"unable to replace \"host-gateway\" of host entry \"host.docker.internal:host-gateway\": host containers internal IP address is empty","message":"preparing container 25ac33a242db7ba451c2eaa100375c03c6876bfd238c62119cb4c5ee66e276b6 for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace \"host-gateway\" of host entry \"host.docker.internal:host-gateway\": host containers internal IP address is empty","response":500}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
```

We are sending HTTP 500 back so compose should know attach failed, it seems like docker-compse bug that they ignore that. But that still does not explain how we can then even get output of the container, I am super confused how docker-compose makes this work...

Looking at server logs I can also only see one attach endpoint call and that is correctly logged as failed.
```
INFO[0004] Request Failed(Internal Server Error): preparing container 2c66a961a2abf184ca4b626eb7e91b26387a456e79321e98f339db2d2e969e66 for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "host.docker.internal:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty 
ERRO[0004] Error attaching to container 2c66a961a2abf184ca4b626eb7e91b26387a456e79321e98f339db2d2e969e66: preparing container 2c66a961a2abf184ca4b626eb7e91b26387a456e79321e98f339db2d2e969e66 for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "host.docker.internal:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty 
@ - - [16/May/2025:17:40:49 +0200] "POST /v1.41/containers/2c66a961a2abf184ca4b626eb7e91b26387a456e79321e98f339db2d2e969e66/attach?stderr=1&stdin=1&stdout=1&stream=1 HTTP/1.1" 500 435 "" "compose/v2.32.1"
@ - - [16/May/2025:17:40:49 +0200] "GET /v1.41/containers/json?all=1&filters=%7B%22label%22%3A%7B%22com.docker.compose.oneoff%3DFalse%22%3Atrue%2C%22com.docker.compose.project%3Dtest%22%3Atrue%7D%7D HTTP/1.1" 200 1566 "" "compose/v2.32.1"
@ - - [16/May/2025:17:40:49 +0200] "GET /v1.41/containers/json?all=1&filters=%7B%22label%22%3A%7B%22com.docker.compose.config-hash%22%3Atrue%2C%22com.docker.compose.oneoff%3DFalse%22%3Atrue%2C%22com.docker.compose.project%3Dtest%22%3Atrue%2C%22com.docker.compose.service%3Dphp-test%22%3Atrue%7D%7D HTTP/1.1" 200 1566 "" "compose/v2.32.1"
@ - - [16/May/2025:17:40:50 +0200] "POST /v1.41/containers/2c66a961a2abf184ca4b626eb7e91b26387a456e79321e98f339db2d2e969e66/start HTTP/1.1" 204 0 "" "compose/v2.32.1"
...
@ - - [16/May/2025:17:40:49 +0200] "GET /v1.41/containers/2c66a961a2abf184ca4b626eb7e91b26387a456e79321e98f339db2d2e969e66/logs?follow=1&stderr=1&stdout=1&tail= HTTP/1.1" 200 5669 "" "compose/v2.32.1"
```

But then docker-compose uses logs to provide the output.
Searching in docker-compose output I see https://github.com/docker/compose/blob/23fc76a5403541eeb8dbdd638f88424bbb799d97/pkg/compose/attach.go#L171-L176

So effectively they happen to trigger a rather weird bug by ignoring all attach errors. So I think our actual attach part is fine.

> we did not transition it back to Initialized despite the failure, so the Start API call goes ahead and starts the container despite the earlier failure.

I think this is the problem then we must fix.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
